blocks.text: July 23rd
Woke up in the forest.
Then I went to the nearest place I could buy coffee.
I drank 4 coffees and then went to a super market.
On of the workers there happened to be a cyclist and told me a better way up the mountain I was going to.
I listened and had a big 900m climb for about 10 kilometres.
On the climb I met another cyclist from Milan who had a vacation home in the alps.
He told me a route he loves over another mountain.
I said I would give it a look.
I went to a town called Aprica.
There I sat for a while and ate pizza.
I thought long and hard about climbing the hill.
I would have to sleep up at 2000metres if I did that.
My sleeping bag is rated for 7 degrees Celsius. I couldn't find any information on the weather other than about 500 metres below it gets to 10C at night.
I decided I would try and if it was really bad I would not sleep at such a high elevation again in the future.
I went to an outdoor store and bought some long underwear to help me stay warm at night.
I went up the climb slow and steady.
It was difficult of course. By the time I leave the alps I will be either destroyed or really fit.
I made it to the top of the hill and was too tired to keep biking so I just cooked dinner and found a pretty spot to camp for the night and watch the sun set over the mountains.
I'm about to go to sleep now and it's already getting chillier. Let's see how it goes. Hopefully I'm not tooo cold tonight :)
Morning update,
It definitely got colder last night. It's hard to say now cold it got. Maybe 8 or 9 degrees.
With long pants, socks. A hoodie, rain coat and a towel on. I slept the entire night very warm. I think even if it went a few degrees colder I would have been fine.
oh this really loud animal was screaming all night. I put earplugs on and that solved the issue for me lol.
The alps are sooooo pretty. I'm loving it.
By for now,
Erik
